human_review_prompts: In five seconds, can the operator tell whether work is blocked?
Does the dashboard make the teacher look organized and credible?
Is the next action visually obvious without reading receipt JSON?
render_checks: 360px mobile scan shows blocker and next action cards
desktop scan shows progress lanes and latest export without horizontal overflow
operator prompt and receipt links remain visible and legible

accessibility.focus_visibility: high contrast focus ring on links, cards, and controls
accessibility.target_size: large mobile touch zones and readable spacing for phone review

color_system.contrast_rule: all text and status labels target WCAG 2.2 AA contrast; color never carries meaning alone
color_system.palette_strategy: deep navy surfaces with semantic green, blue, amber, and red status accents

design_intent.credibility_target: make the teacher-operator look organized, prepared, and technically credible when showing the workflow
design_intent.desired_impression: premium command center with calm high-confidence status clarity
design_intent.engagement_strategy: use grouped status cards, visual progress lanes, and clear next-action hierarchy so the operator sees state within seconds

design_system.basis: Material 3-inspired dark operator dashboard with MetaBlooms governance tokens
component_rules: status cards expose blockers, bootability, focus, and next action
progress lanes use labeled segmented bars with accessible text
decision cards separate required and optional work
receipt links remain visually secondary but discoverable

interaction_feedback.audio_visual_sync: not audio-first; TTS-visible labels can be read without relying on emoji names
interaction_feedback.correct_incorrect_states: pass, blocked, active, and next states use icon, label, border, and text together
interaction_feedback.hover_focus_active: visible focus ring and card lift for keyboard/pointer review
interaction_feedback.progress_state: segmented progress lanes and current-stage badge show completion at a glance

layout.empty_state_rule: unknown values render as explicit unknown cards rather than blank holes
layout.structure: single-column mobile first with responsive cards, progress lanes, export panel, and decision panel
layout.visual_hierarchy: top hero and four status cards answer the current state before receipts and technical details

mobile_first.thumb_reach_rule: status and next-action cards stack for one-handed phone scanning

motion.purpose: motion is limited to state affordance and hover/focus emphasis so progress remains readable

teacher_professional_presentation.dashboard_readability: at-a-glance status cards and progress lanes prioritize what is done, active, blocked, and next
teacher_professional_presentation.professional_language: copy uses concise governance language and avoids placeholder, jokey, or confusing developer text
teacher_professional_presentation.shareable_output_quality: screenshot-ready dashboard cards use clean labels, readable spacing, and no raw debug-first presentation
teacher_professional_presentation.teacher_mode_separation: operator-only governance evidence is separated from any future student-facing classroom artifacts
teacher_professional_presentation.teacher_public_credibility: operator dashboard should make the teacher look prepared, modern, technically organized, and professionally intentional

typography.font_strategy: system sans stack for fast offline single-file rendering
typography.readability_rule: short labels, visible hierarchy, no dense debug paragraphs in primary scan zone
typography.scale: clamped mobile-first heading scale with readable card labels
